title: Conduit CDEvents Base Event
description: Base schema for all Conduit CDEvents. All Conduit event types extend this structure. Does not include customData or data — all event-specific payload belongs in subject.content.

context.description: Routing and identification envelope. Contains only the metadata Conduit needs to route, version, and validate an event — no semantic payload.

version.description: CDEvents specification version this event conforms to.

id.description: Unique identifier for this event instance. MUST be a UUID.

source.description: URI-reference identifying the system that produced this event.

type.description: Event type. For the Conduit base event this MUST be exactly: dev.cdevents.event.base.<major>.<minor>.<patch>.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp indicating when this event occurred.

schemaUri.description: Absolute URI pointing to the JSON Schema that validates this event type.

subject.description: The entity this event is about. All semantic payload — both identity fields and event-specific attributes — lives here.

id.description: Identifier of the subject this event relates to. MUST be non-empty.

type.description: Type label for the subject entity (e.g. pipelineRun, taskRun, artifact).

source.description: URI-reference identifying the subject's origin system, if different from context.source.

content.description: Event-type-specific payload. All semantic data introduced by a derived schema lives here. If a concept key (e.g. 'url') must appear more than once at the same level in this object, it MUST be suffixed with an underscore and a qualifier identifying what it refers to (e.g. 'url_artifact', 'url_pipelineRun'). If the top-level keys are already semantically distinct objects (e.g. 'artifact', 'pipelineRun'), each may carry its own 'url' key internally without namespacing — the parent key provides the disambiguation.
